heading: Tax responsibilities of expatriation
label: 877A Tax responsibilities of expatriation

children.heading: Mark to market
children.label: 1 Mark to market
children.text: All property of a covered expatriate shall be treated as sold on the day before the expatriation date for its fair market value.

children.label: A
children.text: notwithstanding any other provision of this title, any gain arising from such sale shall be taken into account for the taxable year of the sale, and

children.label: B
children.text: any loss arising from such sale shall be taken into account for the taxable year of the sale to the extent otherwise provided by this title, except that section 1091 shall not apply to any such loss.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The amount which would (but for this paragraph) be includible in the gross income of any individual by reason of paragraph (1) shall be reduced (but not below zero) by $600,000.

children.label: I
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: II
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, by substituting “calendar year 2007” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.heading: Rounding
children.label: ii Rounding
children.text: If any amount as adjusted under clause (i) is not a multiple of $1,000, such amount shall be rounded to the nearest multiple of $1,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If the taxpayer elects the application of this subsection with respect to any property treated as sold by reason of subsection (a), the time for payment of the additional tax attributable to such property shall be extended until the due date of the return for the taxable year in which such property is disposed of (or, in the case of property disposed of in a transaction in which gain is not recognized in whole or in part, until such other date as the Secretary may prescribe).

children.heading: Determination of tax with respect to property
children.label: 2 Determination of tax with respect to property
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the additional tax attributable to any property is an amount which bears the same ratio to the additional tax imposed by this chapter for the taxable year solely by reason of subsection (a) as the gain taken into account under subsection (a) with respect to such property bears to the total gain taken into account under subsection (a) with respect to all property to which subsection (a) applies.

children.heading: Termination of extension
children.label: 3 Termination of extension
children.text: The due date for payment of tax may not be extended under this subsection later than the due date for the return of tax imposed by this chapter for the taxable year which includes the date of death of the expatriate (or, if earlier, the time that the security provided with respect to the property fails to meet the requirements of paragraph (4), unless the taxpayer corrects such failure within the time specified by the Secretary).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No election may be made under paragraph (1) with respect to any property unless adequate security is provided with respect to such property.

children.label: i
children.text: it is a bond which is furnished to, and accepted by, the Secretary, which is conditioned on the payment of tax (and interest thereon), and which meets the requirements of section 6325, or

children.label: ii
children.text: it is another form of security for such payment (including letters of credit) that meets such requirements as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: Waiver of certain rights
children.label: 5 Waiver of certain rights
children.text: No election may be made under paragraph (1) unless the taxpayer makes an irrevocable waiver of any right under any treaty of the United States which would preclude assessment or collection of any tax imposed by reason of this section.

children.heading: Elections
children.label: 6 Elections
children.text: An election under paragraph (1) shall only apply to property described in the election and, once made, is irrevocable.

children.heading: Interest
children.label: 7 Interest
children.text: For purposes of section 6601, the last date for the payment of tax shall be determined without regard to the election under this subsection.
any deferred compensation item (as defined in subsection (d)(4)),
any specified tax deferred account (as defined in subsection (e)(2)), and
any interest in a nongrantor trust (as defined in subsection (f)(3)).

children.heading: Treatment of deferred compensation items
children.label: d Treatment of deferred compensation items

children.heading: Withholding on eligible deferred compensation items
children.label: 1 Withholding on eligible deferred compensation items

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any eligible deferred compensation item, the payor shall deduct and withhold from any taxable payment to a covered expatriate with respect to such item a tax equal to 30 percent thereof.

children.heading: Taxable payment
children.label: B Taxable payment
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “taxable payment” means with respect to a covered expatriate any payment to the extent it would be includible in the gross income of the covered expatriate if such expatriate continued to be subject to tax as a citizen or resident of the United States. A deferred compensation item shall be taken into account as a payment under the preceding sentence when such item would be so includible.

children.label: i
children.text: with respect to any deferred compensation item to which clause (ii) does not apply, an amount equal to the present value of the covered expatriate’s accrued benefit shall be treated as having been received by such individual on the day before the expatriation date as a distribution under the plan, and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to any deferred compensation item referred to in paragraph (4)(D), the rights of the covered expatriate to such item shall be treated as becoming transferable and not subject to a substantial risk of forfeiture on the day before the expatriation date,

children.label: B
children.text: no early distribution tax shall apply by reason of such treatment, and

children.label: C
children.text: appropriate adjustments shall be made to subsequent distributions from the plan to reflect such treatment.

children.label: i
children.text: a United States person, or

children.label: ii
children.text: a person who is not a United States person but who elects to be treated as a United States person for purposes of paragraph (1) and meets such requirements as the Secretary may provide to ensure that the payor will meet the requirements of paragraph (1), and

children.label: i
children.text: notifies the payor of his status as a covered expatriate, and

children.label: ii
children.text: makes an irrevocable waiver of any right to claim any reduction under any treaty with the United States in withholding on such item.

children.label: A
children.text: any interest in a plan or arrangement described in section 219(g)(5),

children.label: B
children.text: any interest in a foreign pension plan or similar retirement arrangement or program,

children.label: C
children.text: any item of deferred compensation, and

children.label: D
children.text: any property, or right to property, which the individual is entitled to receive in connection with the performance of services to the extent not previously taken into account under section 83 or in accordance with section 83.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 5 Exception
children.text: Paragraphs (1) and (2) shall not apply to any deferred compensation item to the extent attributable to services performed outside the United States while the covered expatriate was not a citizen or resident of the United States.

children.heading: Application of withholding rules
children.label: A Application of withholding rules
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subchapter B of chapter 3 shall apply for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Application of tax
children.label: B Application of tax
children.text: Any item subject to the withholding tax imposed under paragraph (1) shall be subject to tax under section 871.

children.heading: Coordination with other withholding requirements
children.label: C Coordination with other withholding requirements
children.text: Any item subject to withholding under paragraph (1) shall not be subject to withholding under section 1441 or chapter 24.

children.heading: Treatment of specified tax deferred accounts
children.label: e Treatment of specified tax deferred accounts
A
children.text: the covered expatriate shall be treated as receiving a distribution of his entire interest in such account on the day before the expatriation date,

children.label: B
children.text: no early distribution tax shall apply by reason of such treatment, and

children.label: C
children.text: appropriate adjustments shall be made to subsequent distributions from the account to reflect such treatment.

children.heading: Specified tax deferred account
children.label: 2 Specified tax deferred account
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term “specified tax deferred account” means an individual retirement plan (as defined in section 7701(a)(37)) other than any arrangement described in subsection (k) or (p) of section 408, a qualified tuition program (as defined in section 529), a qualified ABLE program (as defined in section 529A), a Coverdell education savings account (as defined in section 530), a health savings account (as defined in section 223), and an Archer MSA (as defined in section 220).

children.heading: Special rules for nongrantor trusts
children.label: f Special rules for nongrantor trusts
A
children.text: the trustee shall deduct and withhold from such distribution an amount equal to 30 percent of the taxable portion of the distribution, and

children.label: B
children.text: if the fair market value of such property exceeds its adjusted basis in the hands of the trust, gain shall be recognized to the trust as if such property were sold to the expatriate at its fair market value.

children.heading: Taxable portion
children.label: 2 Taxable portion
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “taxable portion” means, with respect to any distribution, that portion of the distribution which would be includible in the gross income of the covered expatriate if such expatriate continued to be subject to tax as a citizen or resident of the United States.

children.heading: Nongrantor trust
children.label: 3 Nongrantor trust
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “nongrantor trust” means the portion of any trust that the individual is not considered the owner of under subpart E of part I of subchapter J. The determination under the preceding sentence shall be made immediately before the expatriation date.

children.label: A
children.text: rules similar to the rules of subsection (d)(6) shall apply, and

children.label: B
children.text: the covered expatriate shall be treated as having waived any right to claim any reduction under any treaty with the United States in withholding on any distribution to which paragraph (1)(A) applies unless the covered expatriate agrees to such other treatment as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Application
children.label: 5 Application
children.text: This subsection shall apply to a nongrantor trust only if the covered expatriate was a beneficiary of the trust on the day before the expatriation date.

children.heading: Definitions and special rules relating to expatriation
children.label: g Definitions and special rules relating to expatriation

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “covered expatriate” means an expatriate who meets the requirements of subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) of section 877(a)(2).

children.label: I
children.text: became at birth a citizen of the United States and a citizen of another country and, as of the expatriation date, continues to be a citizen of, and is taxed as a resident of, such other country, and

children.label: II
children.text: has been a resident of the United States (as defined in section 7701(b)(1)(A)(ii)) for not more than 10 taxable years during the 15-taxable year period ending with the taxable year during which the expatriation date occurs, or

children.label: I
children.text: the individual’s relinquishment of United States citizenship occurs before such individual attains age 18½, and

children.label: II
children.text: the individual has been a resident of the United States (as so defined) for not more than 10 taxable years before the date of relinquishment.

children.heading: Covered expatriates also subject to tax as citizens or residents
children.label: C Covered expatriates also subject to tax as citizens or residents
children.text: In the case of any covered expatriate who is subject to tax as a citizen or resident of the United States for any period beginning after the expatriation date, such individual shall not be treated as a covered expatriate during such period for purposes of subsections (d)(1) and (f) and section 2801.

children.label: A
children.text: any United States citizen who relinquishes his citizenship, and

children.label: B
children.text: any long-term resident of the United States who ceases to be a lawful permanent resident of the United States (within the meaning of section 7701(b)(6)).

children.label: A
children.text: the date an individual relinquishes United States citizenship, or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a long-term resident of the United States, the date on which the individual ceases to be a lawful permanent resident of the United States (within the meaning of section 7701(b)(6)).

children.label: A
children.text: the date the individual renounces his United States nationality before a diplomatic or consular officer of the United States pursuant to paragraph (5) of section 349(a) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (),8 U.S.C. 1481(a)(5)

children.label: B
children.text: the date the individual furnishes to the United States Department of State a signed statement of voluntary relinquishment of United States nationality confirming the performance of an act of expatriation specified in paragraph (1), (2), (3), or (4) of section 349(a) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (–(4)),8 U.S.C. 1481(a)(1)

children.label: C
children.text: the date the United States Department of State issues to the individual a certificate of loss of nationality, or

children.label: D
children.text: the date a court of the United States cancels a naturalized citizen’s certificate of naturalization.

children.heading: Long-term resident
children.label: 5 Long-term resident
children.text: The term “long-term resident” has the meaning given to such term by section 877(e)(2).

children.heading: Early distribution tax
children.label: 6 Early distribution tax
children.text: The term “early distribution tax” means any increase in tax imposed under section 72(t), 220(f)(4), 223(f)(4), 409A(a)(1)(B), 529(c)(6), 529A(c)(3), or 530(d)(4).

children.label: A
children.text: any time period for acquiring property which would result in the reduction in the amount of gain recognized with respect to property disposed of by the taxpayer shall terminate on the day before the expatriation date, and

children.label: B
children.text: any extension of time for payment of tax shall cease to apply on the day before the expatriation date and the unpaid portion of such tax shall be due and payable at the time and in the manner prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Step-up in basis
children.label: 2 Step-up in basis
children.text: Solely for purposes of determining any tax imposed by reason of subsection (a), property which was held by an individual on the date the individual first became a resident of the United States (within the meaning of section 7701(b)) shall be treated as having a basis on such date of not less than the fair market value of such property on such date. The preceding sentence shall not apply if the individual elects not to have such sentence apply. Such an election, once made, shall be irrevocable.

children.heading: Coordination with section 684
children.label: 3 Coordination with section 684
children.text: If the expatriation of any individual would result in the recognition of gain under section 684, this section shall be applied after the application of section 684.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: i Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section.
